message:
fix: prevent SQLite creation when Dolt backend is configured (gt-g5nvc, gt-r1nex)

Two fixes to prevent Beads Classic contamination in Dolt-configured workspaces:

1. checkExistingBeadsData now detects server-mode Dolt installations.
   Previously it only checked for a populated dolt/ directory, which is
   empty in server mode. This allowed bd init (without --backend dolt)
   to bypass the safety check and create a SQLite database alongside
   an existing Dolt configuration. (gt-g5nvc)

2. Auto-bootstrap (cold-start JSONL import) now checks metadata.json
   backend before creating a database. If backend is dolt, it errors
   out instead of silently creating SQLite. This prevents the cycle
   where cleanup removes beads.db, then bd list recreates it from
   the JSONL that is still present. (gt-r1nex)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
